_metadata.title: BCI Security Hardrails
_metadata.description: Security Hardrails = Guardrails (ethical/regulatory constraints) + Hardening (technical enforcement). This file defines the combined defense-in-depth model for BCI security. Guardrails prevent overclaims and ethical violations. Hardening prevents data leakage, injection, and unauthorized access. Together they form hardrails — the rails that keep BCI systems on track.
_metadata.concept: The term 'hardrails' is intentional: guard + hard = hardrail. Guardrails are soft constraints (policy, ethics, documentation). Hardening is enforcement (pattern matching, sanitization, access control). A hardrail is a constraint that is both ethically grounded AND technically enforced.

guardrails.description: Ethical and regulatory constraints that define what the system should NOT do. Derived from published neuroethics literature and regulatory frameworks.
guardrails.source: guardrails.json (8 neuroethics guardrails from Morse, Poldrack, Racine, Ienca, Kellmeyer, Wexler, Tennison, Vul/Eklund)
guardrails.enforcement: Neuromodesty check skill scans output for violations. Violations are flagged and corrected before output.

layers.name: Neuroethics Guardrails
layers.description: 8 published neuroethics constraints (G1-G8) that prevent overclaims about neural systems

layers.name: Regulatory Compliance Guardrails
layers.description: GDPR, CCPA, Chile Neurorights, UNESCO, Mind Act requirements mapped to BCI operations

layers.name: Status Qualifier Guardrails
layers.description: All QIF components (TARA, NISS, NSP, Cs) must be presented as proposed/unvalidated/in-development
layers.enforcement: Mandatory disclaimer in all report output

layers.name: Dual-Use Framing Guardrail
layers.description: Every threat description must be paired with defensive controls. Offensive applications are out of scope.
layers.enforcement: Guardrail G7 check in neuromodesty-check + threat-modeler report format

hardening.description: Technical enforcement mechanisms that detect, prevent, and report security violations. These are the 'hard' in hardrails — pattern matching, sanitization, access control.

layers.name: PII Detection Engine
layers.description: 18 regex patterns detecting direct identifiers, neural identifiers, quasi-identifiers, health data, consent gaps, and retention violations in BCI data pipelines
detection_categories: Neural identifiers (brain fingerprints, cognitive state classification, raw neural export)

layers.name: Report Sanitization Engine
layers.description: 7-rule sanitization applied to all plugin output before it reaches the user
layers.enforcement: Mandatory sanitization rules in bci-scan, bci-threat-model, bci-compliance

layers.name: Prompt Injection Defense
layers.description: All scanned file content treated as untrusted data. Detection of instruction-like patterns in data fields.
layers.enforcement: Untrusted Input Rule in all skills and commands

layers.name: Neural Data Consent Gate
layers.description: Pause and confirm before scanning actual neural data files. Code scanning proceeds without gate.

layers.name: Credential Redaction (Zero-Tolerance)
layers.description: API keys, tokens, passwords, and secrets found in scanned code are ALWAYS replaced with [REDACTED]. This rule has no opt-out, no override, no exception.
layers.enforcement: Hardcoded in all report generation flows

layers.name: Neural Data File Hook
layers.description: PostToolUse hook that triggers when neural data files are written, warning about anonymization and consent metadata
layers.enforcement: hooks.json PostToolUse on Write|Edit for neural file extensions

compliance_report.description: The compliance report combines guardrail and hardening findings into a single assessment
components: PII scan results (HL-01) mapped to regulatory requirements (GL-02)
Technical security findings (existing 3 scan rules) mapped to compliance domains
Neuroethics compliance status (GL-01) for any text output
Status qualifier verification (GL-03) for framework references
Dual-use framing check (GL-04) for threat descriptions
compliance_report.output: Structured Markdown report with executive summary, findings matrix, regulatory mapping, and remediation roadmap
